misconceptions.title: Misinterpreting Inequality Symbols
misconceptions.description: Students incorrectly match strict inequalities (> or <) with inclusive inequality symbols (≥ or ≤). They may not recognize the importance of dashed versus solid lines to indicate these distinctions.

misconceptions.title: Switching Variables With Wrong Signs
misconceptions.description: Students switch the roles of x and y or flip the signs either by misunderstanding substitutions or by procedural errors in solving systems of equations. This results in incorrect solutions to the system.

misconceptions.title: Incorrect Graphical Representation
misconceptions.description: When converting standard form to slope-intercept form, students graph equations without converting, often assuming initial coefficients represent slope and intercept.

misconceptions.title: Incorrect Operations for Solving
misconceptions.description: Mistakes occur using wrong inverse operations, such as adding instead of subtracting, leading to incorrect solutions for equations.
